# SPM — Skills Package Manager

> SPM is a package manager for AI agent skills. Think "npm for agent skills."
> Browse, install, and publish reusable skill packages that extend what AI agents can do.

## What is SPM?

SPM (Skills Package Manager) is a registry and CLI for distributing AI agent skills.
Skills are packaged capabilities — like PDF manipulation, frontend design, or data visualization —
that agents can install and use. Each skill is a `.skl` package containing a SKILL.md file
with instructions, metadata, and optional assets.

## Website

- Homepage: https://skillpkg.dev
- Search skills: https://skillpkg.dev/search
- Browse categories: https://skillpkg.dev/categories
- View a skill: https://skillpkg.dev/skills/{skill-name}
- Author profiles: https://skillpkg.dev/authors/{username}

## API

The SPM registry API is available at https://registry.skillpkg.dev

Key endpoints:
- GET /skills — List/search skills (query params: q, category, trust_tier, sort, limit, offset)
- GET /skills/:name — Get skill details (includes README, versions, security info)
- GET /categories — List all categories with skill counts
- GET /trending?tab=featured|rising|most-installed|new — Trending skills
- GET /authors/:username — Author profile and published skills

## MCP Server

SPM provides an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for programmatic access from AI agents.
Install via: npm install -g @spm/mcp

Available tools:
- spm_search — Search the skill registry
- spm_info — Get detailed info about a specific skill
- spm_categories — List available categories

## CLI

Install the CLI: npm install -g spm

Common commands:
- spm search <query> — Search for skills
- spm info <name> — View skill details
- spm install <name> — Install a skill
- spm publish — Publish a skill to the registry

## Categories

Skills are organized into categories:
- Documents — PDF, DOCX, text processing
- Data & Visualization — Charts, dashboards, analytics
- Frontend — React, UI components, design
- Backend — APIs, databases, servers
- Infrastructure — Docker, CI/CD, cloud
- Testing — Test frameworks, coverage, benchmarks
- Code Quality — Linting, formatting, refactoring
- Security — Auth, encryption, vulnerability scanning
- Productivity — Git, automation, workflow tools

## Trust Tiers

Skills are assigned trust tiers indicating verification level:
- Official — Published by known organizations (Anthropic, Vercel)
- Verified — Author identity verified via GitHub
- Scanned — Passed automated security scanning
- Registered — Published by a registered user
